hypothesis: If SFT-trained open-weight LLMs are fine-tuned with Direct Preference Optimization using thermodynamics-based hierarchical rewards and β=0.5, then they will generate BCC/B2 alloy compositions with higher mean reward, greater compositional validity, improved coverage and novelty, and higher unique-pair generation rates than SFT-only models and baseline methods because DPO aligns generations with physics-based synthesis preferences while limiting distribution shift.
